definitions.text: Containing the maximum possible amount that can fit in the space available.
examples: The jugs were full to the point of overflowing.
Our book gives full treatment to the subject of angling.

definitions.text: Surjective as a map of morphisms
Fully; quite; very; thoroughly; completely; exactly; entirely.
Utmost measure or extent; highest state or degree; the state, position, or moment of fullness; fill.
examples: I was fed to the full.

definitions.text: The phase of the moon when its entire face is illuminated, full moon.
A flip involving a complete turn in midair.
To become full or wholly illuminated.
To make cloth denser and firmer by soaking, beating and pressing; to waulk or walk.
